goal: Land the G8 (Testing+CI) external-binary plugin manifest for Phase 3 Week 11 ‚Äî `plugins/testing/mcp-pytest-runner/plugin.toml` (P3-W11-F08) ‚Äî plus its devtools install hint, an end-to-end `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs` integration test exercising a real `PluginManager::health_check_with_timeout` round-trip via `uvx mcp-pytest-runner@0.2.1`, and a replacement for the TODO-stub acceptance script at `scripts/verify/P3-W11-F08.sh` that pairs an MCP `tools/list` smoke with a tool-level pytest-discover + selective-run smoke against `tests/fixtures/python-project`. Direct port of the WO-0072 solo-manifest template (codegraphcontext G4, single-feature shape) layered with WO-0076 G7 paired-manifest discipline (W1 tokio::process::Command pre-emptive carve-out, live-tools/list capture protocol, UCIL_SKIP_<GROUP>_PLUGIN_E2E env var). Establishes the new `testing.*` capability namespace under `plugins/testing/` (parallel to `quality.*`, `context.*`, `knowledge.*`, `architecture.*`, `search.*`, `navigate.*`, `platform.*`). Per WO-0073/0074/0075/0076 lesson ¬ßplanner: `G6 ‚Üí G7 ‚Üí G8 paired-manifest template port ‚Äî apply same shape to G8`. P3-W11-F07 (test-runner-mcp) is EXPLICITLY EXCLUDED from this WO per DEC-0021 ‚Äî no canonical upstream test-runner-mcp server is published to PyPI/npm at this date; F07 is deferred to Phase 4/7 hardening pending upstream emergence.
plan_summary: Mirror the WO-0072 G4 codegraphcontext solo-manifest precedent verbatim, retargeted from Architecture to Testing group, while applying the WO-0076 W1 + live-tools/list + UCIL_SKIP_<GROUP>_PLUGIN_E2E discipline carry-overs. One stdio MCP server ‚Äî mcp-pytest-runner ‚Äî gets a hand-authored TOML manifest under a new `plugins/testing/mcp-pytest-runner/plugin.toml` directory tree, pinned to the verified-published-immutable upstream tag `mcp-pytest-runner@0.2.1` (PyPI; latest publish 2025-12+, summary 'MCP server providing opinionated pytest execution interface for AI agents') with `transport.type = "stdio"` and a documented `capabilities.provides` list under the `testing.*` namespace. One devtools install script (`scripts/devtools/install-mcp-pytest-runner.sh`) mirrors the `install-mem0-mcp.sh` / `install-semgrep-mcp.sh` shape verbatim ‚Äî informational-only, idempotent, optional warm-up via `--help`. A new `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs` integration test file declares one `mod g8_plugin_manifests { ... }` at file root with one `#[tokio::test] async fn` health-check test ‚Äî substring selector `g8_plugin_manifests::mcp_pytest_runner_manifest_health_check` MUST resolve cleanly per DEC-0007 + WO-0069/0072/0074/0075/0076 lessons ¬ß"For planner: module-root placement". One `scripts/verify/P3-W11-F08.sh` script replaces the TODO stub and runs the targeted `cargo test` selector plus a tool-level smoke (gated by `UCIL_SKIP_TESTING_PLUGIN_E2E` per the WO-0074/0075/0076 `UCIL_SKIP_<GROUP>_PLUGIN_E2E` precedent, defaulting to RUN). The M1/M2 mutation contract is pre-baked verbatim from WO-0076 ¬ß10/¬ß11: M1 poisons `transport.command` (uvx ‚Üí ENOENT), M2 replaces the expected canonical tool name in the test file (executor MUST confirm exact tool names from live `tools/list` BEFORE choosing the literal, per WO-0074/0075/0076 lesson ¬ßexecutor #1: `DON'T translate live tools/list names to snake_case when they ship kebab-case` ‚Äî mcp-pytest-runner tool names are TBD-by-live-capture since the upstream README does not enumerate them in this snapshot). No daemon source changes; no `crates/ucil-daemon/src/**` edits permitted. F08 verify-script smoke runs against a tmpdir copy of `tests/fixtures/python-project` (which has `pyproject.toml` + `src/python_project/` + `tests/` carrying `test_evaluator.py`, `test_lexer.py`, `test_parser.py`, `test_scoring.py`) per F08 spec line `pytest hierarchical test discovery and selective re-run by node ID verified on the fixture python-project`. Per WO-0075 ¬ßexecutor lesson W1: ALL `tokio::test` async helpers MUST use `tokio::process::Command` (NOT `std::process::Command`) for fixture-init / git-init / tmpdir-mutation operations ‚Äî pre-emptive carve-out to avoid the WO-0075 W1 critic warning carried verbatim into this WO via AC `! grep -qE 'std::process::Command' crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs`.
scope_in: 1. NEW plugin manifest at `plugins/testing/mcp-pytest-runner/plugin.toml` for the official mcp-pytest-runner published to PyPI at `mcp-pytest-runner@0.2.1` (latest publish 2025-12+ confirmed via PyPI pre-flight: `https://pypi.org/pypi/mcp-pytest-runner/json` returns HTTP 200; versions seen `['0.1.0', '0.2.0', '0.2.1']`; summary `MCP server providing opinionated pytest execution interface for AI agents`; install via `uvx mcp-pytest-runner` per upstream README). Required keys: `[plugin] name="mcp-pytest-runner" category="testing" priority="P0" version="0.2.1"`; `[transport] type="stdio" command="uvx" args=["mcp-pytest-runner@0.2.1"]`; `[capabilities] provides=["testing.pytest.discover", "testing.pytest.run", "testing.pytest.run_node_id"]` (executor: confirm exact tool names by spawning the live binary and capturing the canonical `tools/list` response BEFORE writing the literal ‚Äî prefer kebab-case-as-emitted-by-upstream over snake-case translation per WO-0074/0075/0076 lesson ¬ßexecutor #1; the master-plan ¬ß4.8 / ¬ß5.8 vocabulary `discover` / `run` etc. is the capability NAME independent of the upstream's literal tool name; the PyPI bin entry is implied as a console-script per the package's `pyproject.toml` ‚Äî `uvx mcp-pytest-runner@0.2.1` resolves the canonical entry-point); `[lifecycle] hot_cold=true idle_timeout_minutes=10` mirrors the ast-grep / Context7 / GitHub-MCP / ESLint-MCP pattern; `[capabilities] languages=["python"]` per the upstream's pytest-only scope; `[capabilities] activation.on_language=["python"]` and `activation.on_tool=["check_quality", "review_changes", "run_tests"]` per master-plan ¬ß14.1 line 1455+ shape. Top-of-file rustdoc comment must document: source URL (https://pypi.org/project/mcp-pytest-runner/), PyPI package name (`mcp-pytest-runner`), pinned tag (`0.2.1`), binary launch command (`uvx mcp-pytest-runner@0.2.1`), advertised tools (full live list from the live `tools/list` capture per scope_in #14), transport type (stdio), API key requirements (NONE ‚Äî runs entirely locally; no auth), and the working-directory invariant (the upstream binary runs pytest in the cwd or against an explicitly-passed path ‚Äî the integration test + verify script MUST copy `tests/fixtures/python-project` into a `mktemp -d` tmpdir and invoke from the tmpdir as cwd; NO modifications to the read-only fixture).

2. NEW devtools script at `scripts/devtools/install-mcp-pytest-runner.sh`. Idempotent bash; verifies python3 + uv (or uvx) available; runs `uvx mcp-pytest-runner@0.2.1 --help 2>&1 | head -1` (or analogous warm-up that triggers PyPI fetch + uv cache without blocking on stdin) to ensure the package is in the uv cache for the integration test's first spawn. Must `set -euo pipefail`; must exit 0 on success; must NOT require sudo; must NOT actually install anything (informational scaffolding only ‚Äî mirrors `install-mem0-mcp.sh` / `install-semgrep-mcp.sh` shape verbatim per WO-0069/0076). Document the working-directory invariant (the test/verify script handles cwd, NOT the install script) as a `[NOTE]` block at the bottom (informational only ‚Äî operator-state, NOT actionable by the script). Must NOT install uv if missing (operator-state); EMIT a `[MISSING]` notice if uv/uvx is not on PATH and exit 0 gracefully (per WO-0076 install-semgrep-mcp.sh shape). Top-of-file documents the optional `--help` warm-up flag works for mcp-pytest-runner per upstream PyPI README CLI surface.

3. NEW integration-test file at `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs`. Top of file: mirror existing `g7_plugin_manifests.rs` shape verbatim ‚Äî same imports, same helper-function placement, same tokio runtime flavor declaration, same `//!` rustdoc declaring mocking is FORBIDDEN. CRITICAL CARRY-FORWARD FROM WO-0075/0076 W1 LESSON: ALL fixture-init / cwd-mutation / tmpdir-population helpers in async test bodies MUST use `tokio::process::Command` (NOT `std::process::Command`), per WO-0075/0076 lesson ¬ßexecutor: `In async integration tests, use tokio::process::Command even for fixture-setup helpers... .claude/rules/rust-style.md ¬ßAsync line 23 mandates tokio::process::Command in async paths`. Single `mod g8_plugin_manifests { ... }` block at file root containing ONE `#[tokio::test] async fn` test ‚Äî `mcp_pytest_runner_manifest_health_check`. The test: (a) loads the corresponding `plugins/testing/mcp-pytest-runner/plugin.toml` via the daemon's existing manifest loader (use the same APIs as g3/g4/g5/g6/g7_plugin_manifests.rs); (b) optionally copies `tests/fixtures/python-project` into a `mktemp -d` tmpdir using `tokio::process::Command` (or `tokio::fs::copy` + `walkdir` / `async_walkdir`) IF the upstream binary's `tools/list` requires a cwd that contains a pytest-collectable target (executor confirms during live capture; if upstream `tools/list` is independent of cwd, the tmpdir copy may be deferred to the verify script's tool-level smoke section only); (c) constructs a `PluginManager` (or analogous abstraction) and calls `health_check_with_timeout` (or the equivalent method that round-trips a real MCP `tools/list` over stdio); (d) asserts the live response carries a specific canonical tool name (executor confirms via live capture; e.g. `discover_tests` / `run_tests` / analogous ‚Äî whatever `mcp-pytest-runner@0.2.1` advertises); (e) panics with `(SAn) <semantic_name>; got: <full-live-tool-list>` on assertion failure per DEC-0007 SA-numbered panic-body format. Substring selector `g8_plugin_manifests::mcp_pytest_runner_manifest_health_check` MUST resolve cleanly via `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g8_plugin_manifests <selector>`. NO `mod tests { ... }` nesting; the test function lives at `mod g8_plugin_manifests` ROOT (NOT inside a nested `mod tests` per DEC-0007 + WO-0069/0072/0074/0075/0076 lessons ¬ß"For planner"). Mirror the existing `g7_plugin_manifests.rs` file shape verbatim. Provide a `skip_via_env()` helper checking `UCIL_SKIP_TESTING_PLUGIN_E2E` per WO-0074/0075/0076 lesson ¬ßexecutor #3 (document inline; default to RUN; verifier MUST NOT set the opt-out per scope_in #15).

4. NEW `scripts/verify/P3-W11-F08.sh` (replace TODO stub) for mcp-pytest-runner. `set -euo pipefail`. (a) Sanity-check `plugins/testing/mcp-pytest-runner/plugin.toml` parses as TOML and asserts the keys named in scope_in #1 via `python3 -c 'import tomllib; ...'`. (b) Run `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g8_plugin_manifests g8_plugin_manifests::mcp_pytest_runner_manifest_health_check 2>&1 | tee /tmp/wo-0077-f08-cargo.log` and `grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. 1 passed; 0 failed|1 tests run: 1 passed' /tmp/wo-0077-f08-cargo.log`. (c) Tool-level smoke (per WO-0074/0075/0076 lesson ¬ßexecutor #4: prefer python polling with deadline over bash sleep): copy `tests/fixtures/python-project` into `mktemp -d` tmpdir BEFORE invoking the upstream binary (per WO-0074/0075/0076 lesson ¬ßexecutor #5); spawn `uvx mcp-pytest-runner@0.2.1` from the tmpdir as cwd (the fixture has `pyproject.toml` declaring `tool.pytest.ini_options.testpaths=["tests"]` and `tests/test_evaluator.py`, `test_lexer.py`, `test_parser.py`, `test_scoring.py` with at least dozens of test functions per pre-flight ls); send an MCP `initialize` + `notifications/initialized` + `tools/list` JSON-RPC sequence over stdio (use python with 90-second deadline polling per WO-0074/0076 ¬ßexecutor #4; pytest discovery may take several seconds on cold start); assert `tools/list` response carries the upstream's canonical pytest-discover and pytest-run tool names (executor confirms exact names from live `tools/list`); send a `tools/call` for the discover tool with a path argument pointing at the tmpdir; assert response is a successful JSON-RPC reply and its result contains a non-empty list of pytest node IDs in pytest's canonical `path::ClassName::method_name` or `path::function_name` format (e.g., `tests/test_lexer.py::test_tokenize_simple` or analogous from the fixture's actual tests); send a follow-up `tools/call` for the run tool with a SUBSET of the discovered node IDs (e.g., 1-3 specific node IDs from one test file) ‚Äî assert the response carries structured pytest run results with at least pass/fail counts per F08 spec line `pytest hierarchical test discovery and selective re-run by node ID verified on the fixture python-project`. Gate the entire tool-level smoke section behind `UCIL_SKIP_TESTING_PLUGIN_E2E` env-var per the WO-0074/0075/0076 precedent ‚Äî DEFAULT to RUN. Final exit 0 on success; non-zero on any check failure with a clear `>&2` diagnostic. The cargo-test `tools/list` path is the load-bearing assertion; the tool-level pytest-discover + selective-run smoke is the additional smoke per F08 spec.

5. M1 mutation contract (transport.command poison; runs once). Pre-mutation: `md5sum plugins/testing/mcp-pytest-runner/plugin.toml > /tmp/wo-0077-mcp-pytest-runner-orig.md5` (capturing snapshot BEFORE any edit). Mutation: edit the targeted `plugin.toml` and replace `command = "uvx"` with `command = "/__ucil_test_nonexistent_M1__"`. Selector: `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g8_plugin_manifests g8_plugin_manifests::mcp_pytest_runner_manifest_health_check`. Expected panic: a `PluginError::Spawn { source: Os { code: 2, kind: NotFound } }` chain (or analogous `tokio::process::Command::spawn` ENOENT-class error). Restore: `git checkout -- plugins/testing/mcp-pytest-runner/plugin.toml`. Confirm: `md5sum -c /tmp/wo-0077-mcp-pytest-runner-orig.md5` returns OK. Mirror the WO-0072/0076 ¬ß10 verbatim shape.

6. M2 mutation contract (expected-tool-name regression; runs once). Pre-mutation: `md5sum crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs > /tmp/wo-0077-g8tests-orig.md5`. Mutation: edit the test file and replace whichever string the test asserts as the canonical tool name (executor will have selected it from the live `tools/list` response per scope_in #14) with `"NON_EXISTENT_TOOL_M2"`. Selector: the targeted test. Expected: structured panic carrying the full live tool-list (`(SAn) <semantic>; got: ["<live tool 1>", "<live tool 2>", ...]; want: "NON_EXISTENT_TOOL_M2"`). Restore: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs`. Confirm via md5. Mirror the WO-0072/0076 ¬ß11 verbatim shape.

Use `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-daemon --tests --summary-only --json | jq '.data[0].totals.lines.percent'` as the authoritative measurement (per WO-0074/0075/0076 lesson ¬ßexecutor: the literal `--no-default-features --features default` arg-pair errors because ucil-daemon has no `default` feature defined). WO is purely additive on tests + manifests + scripts ‚Äî pre-WO baseline must be preserved (no `crates/ucil-daemon/src/**` source changes per scope_out #2). Document the measurement in the RFR.

Apply WO-0076 lessons ¬ß"For planner" + ¬ß"For executor" + ¬ß"For verifier" verbatim: tests at module root (NOT inside `mod tests { ... }`); DEC-0007 SA-numbered panic-body format on every assert; DEC-0008 ¬ß4 dependency-inversion seam pattern for any future production-wiring follow-up (NOT in this WO scope ‚Äî this WO is manifests-only); cite WO-0044 + WO-0069 + WO-0072 + WO-0074 + WO-0075 + WO-0076 lessons by ID in the RFR's ¬ß"Lessons applied" section. Specific WO-0076 lessons to apply verbatim: (a) ¬ßexecutor lessons #1-#5 from WO-0075 carry-over (live capture, no --mcp warm-up, UCIL_SKIP env var, python deadline polling, tmpdir copy); (b) WO-0075 ¬ßexecutor W1 pre-emptively applied ‚Äî `! grep -qE 'std::process::Command' crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g8_plugin_manifests.rs` AC enforced; (c) WO-0076 ¬ßplanner: `For external-CLI-dependent MCP servers, document the CLI dep as load-bearing operator-state in scope_in` ‚Äî N/A here since `mcp-pytest-runner` does NOT have an external Python-side CLI dep beyond `pytest` itself which is automatically resolvable via the uvx-managed venv (the upstream package declares pytest as a dependency in its `pyproject.toml`); (d) WO-0076 ¬ßplanner: `For verify scripts that use parent-process env-var mutation, prescribe --test-threads=1 enforcement OR direct Command::env propagation` ‚Äî N/A here since this WO does NOT require parent-process env-var mutation (no SEMGREP_PATH-style operator-state surfacing); (e) WO-0076 ¬ßverifier: ensure UCIL_SKIP_EXTERNAL_PLUGIN_TESTS and UCIL_SKIP_TESTING_PLUGIN_E2E are NOT exported when running cargo test g8_plugin_manifests::* and bash scripts/verify/P3-W11-F08.sh.

Mocking ... is FORBIDDEN` rustdoc declaration in the test file is exempt under WO-0048 line 363 + WO-0072/0075/0076 ¬ßexecutor `Negative assertion forbidding mocking, NOT a mock implementation`); (f) `bash scripts/verify/P3-W11-F08.sh` green; (g) `command -v uvx python3` confirms PATH has both required runtimes ‚Äî the verifier requires the same.

11. Live-tool-name capture protocol (WO-0074/0075/0076 ¬ßexecutor #1 lesson ‚Äî CRITICAL for M2 mutation correctness): BEFORE writing any tool-name assertion literal, the executor MUST: (a) run `uvx mcp-pytest-runner@0.2.1` directly to capture canonical tool names (the canonical npx/uvx command with stdin frames driving past `initialize` ‚Üí `notifications/initialized` ‚Üí `tools/list`); capture the `tools/list` response's `result.tools[].name` array verbatim; (b) embed this list in the manifest's top-of-file rustdoc comment + the integration test's panic-body string. Choose ONE canonical tool name as the M2 assertion target ‚Äî prefer the most-stable / most-documented one (e.g., the upstream's primary discovery tool ‚Äî pytest discovery is the canonical pytest entry point per F08 spec). Document the chosen literal + the full live tool-list in the RFR's `## Live tool name capture` section per the WO-0074/0075/0076 RFR template. CRITICAL: the upstream PyPI README does NOT enumerate the advertised tools in the snapshot the planner has access to ‚Äî the executor's live capture is the ONLY source of truth.

12. Capability namespace establishment (`testing.*`): parallel to `search.*` (probe), `navigate.*` (scip), `knowledge.*` (codebase-memory + mem0), `architecture.*` (codegraphcontext), `context.*` (Context7 + Repomix), `platform.*` (GitHub + Git + Filesystem), `quality.*` (ESLint + Semgrep). G8 Testing sources advertise capabilities under the `testing.*` prefix; subdivision per source: `testing.pytest.*` for the mcp-pytest-runner. Subsequent G8 plugins (test-runner-mcp deferred per DEC-0021; pytest-mcp Pytest-plugin variant per master-plan ¬ß4.8 line 404; any future Vitest/cargo-nextest/Go MCP servers when they emerge) MUST extend this namespace consistently when they land in future WOs.

13. F08 fixture-aware smoke. The fixture `tests/fixtures/python-project` carries `pyproject.toml` (with `[project] name="python-project" requires-python=">=3.11"; [project.optional-dependencies] dev=["pytest>=8.0"]; [tool.pytest.ini_options] testpaths=["tests"]`) + `src/python_project/` + `tests/test_evaluator.py`, `test_lexer.py`, `test_parser.py`, `test_scoring.py` per pre-flight ls. The mcp-pytest-runner needs a working pytest-discoverable tree to produce a non-empty `tools/call discover` response. Executor MUST: (a) copy the fixture into `mktemp -d` tmpdir BEFORE invoking the upstream binary (NOT in the fixture ‚Äî fixture is read-only per `forbidden_paths`); (b) document the fixture-pristineness invariant in the verify script + integration test comments; (c) the F08 spec line `pytest hierarchical test discovery and selective re-run by node ID verified on the fixture python-project` REQUIRES the smoke to actually surface pytest node IDs in canonical form (`path::ClassName::method` or `path::function`) AND a successful selective-run smoke against a SUBSET of those IDs (not the whole suite). Document the chosen approach in the RFR.

14. Forbidden-path audits the executor MUST run BEFORE pushing. Every entry in `forbidden_paths` (scope_out #16) MUST produce `git diff main feat -- <path>` returning empty. Specifically: (a) `git diff main feat -- crates/ucil-daemon/src/` ‚Äî empty (no daemon source changes; this WO is manifests-only); (b) `git diff main feat -- crates/ucil-core/src/` ‚Äî empty; (c) `git diff main feat -- tests/fixtures/` ‚Äî empty (fixtures are read-only; tmpdir copies in tests/scripts are NOT fixtures); (d) `git diff main feat -- ucil-build/feature-list.json ucil-build/feature-list.schema.json ucil-master-plan-v2.1-final.md` ‚Äî empty (oracle docs are immutable); (e) `git diff main feat -- ucil-build/decisions/` ‚Äî empty (DEC-0021 was pre-authored by the planner alongside this WO; ADRs are planner-territory); (f) `git diff main feat -- crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g3_plugin_manifests.rs crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g4_plugin_manifests.rs crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g5_plugin_manifests.rs crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g6_plugin_manifests.rs crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g7_plugin_manifests.rs crates/ucil-daemon/tests/plugin_manifests.rs` ‚Äî empty (prior group tests are isolated per WO-0069 ¬ßexecutor #2 separation invariant); (g) `git diff main feat -- plugins/{architecture,context,knowledge,platform,quality,search,structural}/` ‚Äî empty (other groups' plugins untouched); (h) `git diff main feat -- scripts/verify/P3-W11-F07.sh` ‚Äî empty (F07 is deferred per DEC-0021 ‚Äî its TODO-stub verify script remains unchanged in this WO). Cite WO-0072 scope_in #2 (six-audit checklist) and WO-0075 ¬ßexecutor (post-cherry-pick / post-edit audit shape) as the audit template.

15. Lessons-learned summary the executor MUST embed in the RFR's `## Lessons applied` section (cite each WO + lesson section + rationale): WO-0074/0075/0076 ¬ßplanner: G5 ‚Üí G6 ‚Üí G7 ‚Üí G8 paired-manifest template port (this WO is the G8 application ‚Äî solo-feature shape per WO-0072 codegraphcontext G4 precedent); WO-0074/0075/0076 ¬ßexecutor #1: live tool-name capture before writing assertion literals; WO-0075/0076 ¬ßexecutor W1: tokio::process::Command in async test helpers (pre-emptively applied here to avoid the W1 critic warning); WO-0075/0076 ¬ßexecutor #5: tmpdir copy of fixture before invoking; WO-0074/0076 ¬ßexecutor #4: python deadline polling for tool-call smokes; WO-0067/0068/0069/0070/0072/0073/0074/0075/0076: pre-baked M1/M2 mutation contract; WO-0067/0068/0069/0070: DEC-0007 SA-numbered panic-body format; WO-0067..WO-0076: substantive AC23 coverage standing protocol (env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov); WO-0067..WO-0076: AC30/AC31 phase-1/phase-2 effectiveness-gate flake carry-over with 3 standing escalations; WO-0070..WO-0076: AC25 git log feat ^main --merges = 0 wording. NO P3-W11-F07 (test-runner-mcp plugin) ‚Äî DEFERRED per DEC-0021 to Phase 4/7 hardening pending upstream availability (no canonical PyPI/npm/GitHub test-runner-mcp server published at this date; pre-flight sweep documented in DEC-0021 ¬ßContext).
